mission.title: Install and Configure Argo on Kubernetes
mission.description: Argo CD is a declarative, GitOps continuous delivery tool for Kubernetes, allowing you to manage application definitions, configurations, and environments in a version-controlled manner. Installing Argo CD enables automated deployment and lifecycle management of your applications.

steps.title: Add the Argo CD Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Argo CD Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add argo https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Argo CD using Helm
steps.description:
Install Argo CD in a new namespace called 'argocd' using the following command:
```bash
helm install argocd argo/argo-cd --version v3.3.2 --namespace argocd --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all Argo CD pods are running by executing:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argocd
```

steps.title: Access the Argo CD API Server
steps.description:
To access the Argo CD API server, you can port-forward the service:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/argocd-server -n argocd 8080:443
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Argo CD will show all pods in the 'argocd' namespace as running. You can access the Argo CD UI at http://localhost:8080 after port-forwarding the service.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add argo https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm
helm repo update
helm install argocd argo/argo-cd --version v3.3.2 --namespace argocd --create-namespace

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Argo CD release using Helm to remove the deployment and associated resources. Run the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall argocd --namespace argocd
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent resources that were created during the installation. Execute the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete crd applications.argoproj.io
kubectl delete crd appprojects.argoproj.io
kubectl delete crd repositories.argoproj.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'argocd' namespace to clean up all remaining resources. Verify that the namespace has been removed by running:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace argocd
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of your Argo CD installation. You can export the current configuration with:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argocd -o yaml > argocd-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade Argo CD to the latest version. Run the following commands:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade argocd argo/argo-cd --version v3.4.0 --namespace argocd
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, check that all Argo CD pods are running with the new version. Execute:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argocd
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you see Argo CD pods in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for the affected pod to diagnose the issue:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n argocd
``` 
Look for error messages that indicate configuration issues or missing dependencies.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to access Argo CD API Server
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Argo CD API server, ensure that the port-forward command is running. Check the service status with:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n argocd
``` 
If the service is not found, verify that the installation was successful.

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade fails due to a version conflict, check the current version of the release with:
```bash
helm list --namespace argocd
``` 
Ensure that you are specifying a valid version in the upgrade command.

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits causing OOMKilled
troubleshooting.description:
If Argo CD pods are being killed due to Out Of Memory (OOM) errors, consider increasing the memory limits set in the deployment. Check the pod status with:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n argocd
``` 
Then edit the deployment to adjust the resource limits accordingly.

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed.
